label: 254q–1 Grants to States for loan repayment programs

children.heading: Authority for grants
children.label: 1 Authority for grants
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Administrator of the Health Resources and Services Administration, may make grants to States for the purpose of assisting the States in operating programs described in paragraph (2) in order to provide for the increased availability of primary health care services in health professional shortage areas. The National Advisory Council established under shall advise the Administrator regarding the program under this section.section 254j of this title

children.heading: Loan repayment programs
children.label: 2 Loan repayment programs
children.text: The programs referred to in paragraph (1) are, subject to subsection (c), programs of entering into contracts under which the State involved agrees to pay all or part of the principal, interest, and related expenses of the educational loans of health professionals in consideration of the professionals agreeing to provide primary health services in health professional shortage areas.

children.heading: Direct administration by State agency
children.label: 3 Direct administration by State agency
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant under paragraph (1) unless the State involved agrees that the program operated with the grant will be administered directly by a State agency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant under subsection (a) unless the State agrees that, with respect to the costs of making payments on behalf of individuals under contracts made pursuant to paragraph (2) of such subsection, the State will make available (directly or through donations from public or private entities) non-Federal contributions in cash toward such costs in an amount equal to not less than $1 for each $1 of Federal funds provided in the grant.

children.heading: Determination of amount of non-Federal contribution
children.label: 2 Determination of amount of non-Federal contribution
children.text: In determining the amount of non-Federal contributions in cash that a State has provided pursuant to paragraph (1), the Secretary may not include any amounts provided to the State by the Federal Government.

children.heading: Assignments for health professional shortage areas under Federal program
children.label: 1 Assignments for health professional shortage areas under Federal program
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant under subsection (a) unless the State involved agrees that, in carrying out the program operated with the grant, the State will assign health professionals participating in the program only to public and nonprofit private entities located in and providing health services in health professional shortage areas.

children.heading: Remedies for breach of contracts
children.label: 2 Remedies for breach of contracts
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant under subsection (a) unless the State involved agrees that the contracts provided by the State pursuant to paragraph (2) of such subsection will provide remedies for any breach of the contracts by the health professionals involved.

children.label: i
children.text: the annual amount of payments provided on behalf of the professionals regarding educational loans; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the availability of remedies for any breach of the contracts by the health professionals involved.

children.label: i
children.text: the excess of such annual payments above the maximum amount authorized in section 254–1(g)(2)(A) of this title for annual payments regarding contracts is paid solely from non-Federal contributions under subsection (b); andl

children.label: ii
children.text: the contract provides that the health professional involved will satisfy the requirement of obligated service under the contract solely through the provision of primary health services in a health professional shortage area that is receiving priority for purposes of and that is authorized to receive assignments under of individuals who are participating in the Scholarship Program under section 254 of this title.section 254f–1(a)(1) of this titlesection 254f of this titlel

children.heading: Restrictions on use of funds
children.label: d Restrictions on use of funds
A
children.text: within the State to provide technical or other nonfinancial assistance under subsection (f) of section 254c of this title;1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: under a memorandum of agreement entered into with the State under subsection (h) of such section; or

children.label: C
children.text: under a grant under ; orsection 254r of this title
for any purpose other than making payments on behalf of health professionals under contracts entered into pursuant to subsection (a)(2).
to submit to the Secretary such reports regarding the States loan repayment program, as are determined to be appropriate by the Secretary; and
to submit such a report not later than January 10 of each fiscal year immediately following any fiscal year for which the State has received such a grant.

children.heading: Requirement of application
children.label: f Requirement of application
children.text: The Secretary may not make a grant under subsection (a) unless an application for the grant is submitted to the Secretary and the application is in such form, is made in such manner, and contains such agreements, assurances, and information as the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out such subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may not make payments under subsection (a) to a State for any fiscal year subsequent to the first fiscal year of such payments unless the Secretary determines that, for the immediately preceding fiscal year, the State has complied with each of the agreements made by the State under this section.

children.heading: Reduction in grant relative to number of breached contracts
children.label: 2 Reduction in grant relative to number of breached contracts
A
children.text: Before making a grant under subsection (a) to a State for a fiscal year, the Secretary shall determine the number of contracts provided by the State under paragraph (2) of such subsection with respect to which there has been an initial breach by the health professionals involved during the fiscal year preceding the fiscal year for which the State is applying to receive the grant.

children.label: B
children.text: Subject to paragraph (3), in the case of a State with 1 or more initial breaches for purposes of subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall reduce the amount of a grant under subsection (a) to the State for the fiscal year involved by an amount equal to the sum of the expenditures of Federal funds made regarding the contracts involved and an amount representing interest on the amount of such expenditures, determined with respect to each contract on the basis of the maximum legal rate prevailing for loans made during the time amounts were paid under the contract, as determined by the Treasurer of the United States.

children.heading: Waiver regarding reduction in grant
children.label: 3 Waiver regarding reduction in grant
children.text: The Secretary may waive the requirement established in paragraph (2)(B) with respect to the initial breach of a contract if the Secretary determines that such breach by the health professional involved was attributable solely to the professional having a serious illness.

children.heading: “State” defined
children.label: h “State” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “State” means each of the 50 States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, Palau, the Marshall Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For the purpose of making grants under subsection (a), there are authorized to be appropriated $12,000,000 for fiscal year 2008, and such sums as may be necessary for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2012.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: 2 Availability
children.text: Amounts appropriated under paragraph (1) shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may award grants to States for the purpose of assisting such States in operating loan repayment programs under which such States enter into contracts to repay all or part of the eligible loans borrowed by, or on behalf of, individuals who agree to serve in State, local, or tribal health departments that serve health professional shortage areas or other areas at risk of a public health emergency, as designated by the Secretary.

children.heading: Loans eligible for repayment
children.label: 2 Loans eligible for repayment
children.text: To be eligible for repayment under this subsection, a loan shall be a loan made, insured, or guaranteed by the Federal Government that is borrowed by, or on behalf of, an individual to pay the cost of attendance for a program of education leading to a degree appropriate for serving in a State, local, or tribal health department as determined by the Secretary and the chief executive officer of the State in which the grant is administered, at an institution of higher education (as defined in ), including principal, interest, and related expenses on such loan.section 1002 of title 20

children.label: A
children.text: the requirements of subsections (b), (f), and (g) shall apply to such awards; and

children.label: B
children.text: the requirements of subsection (c) shall apply to such awards except that with respect to paragraph (1) of such subsection, the State involved may assign an individual only to public and nonprofit private entities that serve health professional shortage areas or areas at risk of a public health emergency, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection, such sums as may be necessary for each of fiscal years 2007 through 2010.
